rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERTIE VICTORIA FOSTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent died on 5/1/15, so the first two-year accounting is now very delinquent. Preston Segura is also entitled to all of the AT&T stock, but that was not part of the request for preliminary distribution (only $125,000 in cash was ordered to each residuary beneficiary on 12/17/19). Preston Segura and Carlton Jackson are entitled to the residue of the estate (aside from the real property and the $100,000 specifically devised to the co-executors). There was $687,011.88 in cash inventoried. There should also be "rents and royalties" (according to the co-executors' own allegation in the predeceased husband's estate) now being received by this estate from Louisiana properties. No one representing Preston Segura's interest was party to the settlement agreement approved on 1/30/17, which affected the residue of this estate. His one-half of the residue of this estate should, therefore, not be affected by the settlement agreement. Still nothing has been filed to indicate whose children the two nephews are, despite the Court's instructions at the 6/8/22 hearing that a supplement be filed to address this. If the payment to Alice Livingston has been made pursuant to the settlement agreement, no receipt has been filed. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN MCKANNAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/17/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Does Sarah McKannay Tate not have any issue? Her late-filed disclaimer would result in her interest passing to her issue as though she had predeceased the decedent. If Decedent was survived by grandchildren or more distant issue, they must now be identified and must be given notice. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the legal description of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] What was the character of the real property? [Probate Code Section 8850(c).] Please file a copy of the vesting deed. The surviving spouse (administrator) must affirmatively state that he elected to probate assets that were distributable directly to him. [LRSF 14.35 I.5. and Probate Code Section 13502.] Paragraph 22 of the report contradicts the information on the first page of the petition, which shows the administrator as being self-represented. The creditor claims period expired on 11/15/21, four months after Letters were issued, not 7/25/21. (Notice to the state agencies was given before the administrator, the general personal representative, was appointed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/7/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAULA LIANE BISSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although (1) the "Response" filed on 6/2/22 actually amended the petition, which required notice, (2) the petition caption erroneously indicated that the executor waived her compensation, and (3) it is unclear why the estate had to bear the cost of publishing notice again if the newspaper erroneously published notice in the first place. If the notice sent to the newspaper failed to indicate that a will was being admitted, that cost also should not have been borne by the estate but by the party who failed to send the proper notice information to the newspaper. Responsive pleadings should stand on their own and not be an "answer sheet" to issues raised by an examiner. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution". or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDITH ARMSTRONG NEWMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 08/14/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 08/28/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAXIM CHARLES MARCOTTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. status hearing on close of estate set for 8/14/2023. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed probate referee.

rulings.Case Title: TRUST FOR MICHAEL SWORDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #15, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing by the assigned judicial officer after review.
